scope: Single-review actions (accept/reject/dismiss), promotion, cascade application & journaling, invalidation (eager + lazy), deletion planning. The PRD §11.4 three-mode invalidation and §6.3 deletion logic live here.

tests.name: graft that would create a cycle → 409
graft that would create an in-tree duplicate (diamond) → 409
graft brings a subtree whose names clash with the target tree → 409
happy cross-tree graft → 200; grafted taxon becomes shared
create whose name clashes with an existing sibling → 409
create whose name clashes case-insensitively → 409 (§3.4)
happy create at root level → 200, new taxon attached
accept rename: live taxon name updates, change becomes accepted, dequeued
accept add-create: new taxon owned by accepting reviewer, attached to parent
accept add-graft: edge added, grafted taxon stays owned by original owner (§9.3)
accept detach: edge removed, detached child survives
rename to a name that clashes in the target tree → 409 conflict, change auto-dismissed (§11.4 case 2)
rename clashes in ANOTHER containing tree (not the target) → 409 conflict
rename across two trees both clean → accepted; both trees see new name
rename to the same name (case-insensitive no-op) → accepted
cascade rooted at a queued rename with no descendants accepts only the seed
cascade extends through ownership transfer (Appendix A.1 Urban Fantasy / Paranormal Romance)
cascade stops at boundaries routed to other owners; bounded change stays queued for that owner
cascade rooted at an add-create with no nested ops accepts only the seed
cascade accepts mixed nested ops (rename, add, detach) in payload-topological order
self-routed cascade — reviewer is also the proposer
cascade rolls back on mid-cascade in-tree name clash; live state and bookkeeping restored
cascade rolls back on cross-tree name clash; restores both trees
cascade rolls back on mid-cascade cycle (graft of an ancestor)
cascade rolls back on mid-cascade in-tree duplicate (diamond)
cascade rolls back on first-step failure with nothing applied
§12.2 non-equivalence: same change validates standalone but cascade rolls back when an earlier step would set up the clash
planDeletion: lone root → region [N]
planDeletion: wholly-U-owned subtree → entire subtree in region
planDeletion: halt at other-owned child — region stops before it
planDeletion: stranded U-owned descendant beneath an other-owned halt is NOT in region
planDeletion: precondition 1 — region taxon is shared → violation shared_in_region
planDeletion: precondition 1 — a region descendant is shared into another tree
planDeletion: precondition 2 — N has one foreign-owned parent → violation parent_not_owned
planDeletion: precondition 2 — N has one U-owned parent → ok
planDeletion: an other-owned halt-frontier taxon may itself be shared — does NOT trip precondition 1
dismiss happy path: case-3 invalid change → 200; removed from queue
dismiss on a still-queued (still valid) change → 409 change_not_invalid
dismiss on a case-1 invalid (never queued) change → 409 change_not_in_queue
dismiss on a case-2 (already auto-dismissed) change → 409 change_not_in_queue
dismiss on an accepted change → 409 change_not_invalid
dismiss on a rejected change → 409 change_not_invalid
dismiss auth: null caller → 403
dismiss auth: unregistered caller → 403
dismiss auth: malformed X-Username (NBSP-prefixed) → 400
dismiss on unknown change id → 404
dismiss as non-reviewer → 403
dismiss is idempotent in effect but not in state: second dismiss → 409 change_not_in_queue
§11.4 case 1: an add becoming invalid propagates to its payload descendants
§11.4 case 1: invalidation cascades through nested add chain (A1 → A2 → A3)
§11.4 case 2: successful accept auto-dismisses a sibling that now clashes
§11.4 case 2: failed accept auto-dismisses the change itself (replaces Phase-6 placeholder)
§11.4 case 2 via cascade: cascade succeeds; a sibling queued change broken by it auto-dismisses
§11.4 case 3: direct rename by another user makes a queued rename clash; marked invalid, kept queued
§11.4 case 3: direct detach by another user (Appendix A.2 tail) invalidates a queued create under the grafted taxon
§11.4 case 3: a different reviewer's accept (on a different proposal) invalidates Y's queued change
unrelated writes do NOT eagerly transition a queued change's state — only a lazy seam does
lazy detection on GET /queue: a queued change broken externally surfaces as invalid in queue
lazy detection on review-action entry: case-3 invalidation surfaces for non-targeted queued changes
idempotence: two successive GET /queue with no intervening writes return the same response
Appendix A.1 cascade-shape: accept create → nested create promotes to accepter's queue
nested rename under create routes to renamed-taxon's owner (not accepter)
nested add under graft promotes to grafted-taxon's owner (§9.3)
nested detach under graft promotes to grafted-taxon's owner
only direct dependents promote: deeper add stays latent until its own parent accepts
sibling dependents under one accepted add all promote in change-id order
existence dep broken between submission and graft accept → dependent invalidated
reject rename: change marked rejected, live name unchanged, dequeued
reject detach: edge survives, no descendants to worry about
reject add-create with no nested ops: state goes rejected, no taxon created
reject add-create with nested create: outer rejected, inner invalid with reason citing outer
reject add-graft with mixed nested ops: all descendants invalidated
multi-level cascade: rejecting an outer add invalidates ALL nested descendants
reject of a rename DOES NOT invalidate its descendants (decision #1 keystone)
reject of a detach: detach has no children so propagation is moot; siblings untouched
POST /changes/:id/accept: auth + precondition triplet
null caller → 403 forbidden
unregistered caller → 403 forbidden
unknown change id → 404 change-not-found
non-reviewer caller → 403 not-reviewer
case-different caller passes reviewer check
non-queued (latent) → 409 change-not-queued
POST /changes/:id/accept: rename success
rename applies to live graph and transitions to accepted
POST /changes/:id/accept: add-create success
mints new taxon owned by caller and adds edge
accepting reviewer becomes owner (not the proposer)
acceptedBy preserves registry casing regardless of header casing
POST /changes/:id/accept: add-graft success
adds edge to live graph
POST /changes/:id/accept: detach success
removes edge from live graph (no invariant check)
POST /changes/:id/accept: failure → invalid + stays in queue
invariant violation (name clash) → 409 accept-invariant-violation; change invalid in queue
retry on invalid change still refuses with change-not-queued
precondition failure (target deleted) → 409 accept-precondition-failed
failed add-create does not insert a zombie taxon
failed accept does not mutate live graph
POST /changes/:id/accept: response shape
response status field matches GET /proposals/{id}
applyAcceptedChange — rename
success: renames the live taxon
failure on in-tree name clash: violation returned, no mutation
applyAcceptedChange — add-graft
success: adds the edge
failure on cycle: violation returned, edge not added
applyAcceptedChange — add-create
success: inserts the new taxon with the accepting reviewer as owner and adds the edge
failure on in-tree name clash: violation returned, no taxon inserted
applyAcceptedChange — detach
success: removes the edge
detach never returns a violation (subtractive on edges)
POST /changes/:id/accept-cascade — auth and preconditions
null caller → 403 forbidden
unknown change id → 404 change-not-found
non-reviewer → 403 not-reviewer
non-queued (rejected) → 409 change-not-queued
POST /changes/:id/accept-cascade — single-change cascade
queued rename with no dependents behaves like single-accept
POST /changes/:id/accept-cascade — multi-step frontier extension
cascades through nested add-creates routed to same reviewer
POST /changes/:id/accept-cascade — rollback on failure
mid-cascade in-tree-duplicate rollback restores pre-cascade state
POST /changes/:id/accept-cascade — halts at other-reviewer
does not fold a dependent routed to a different reviewer
POST /changes/:id/accept-cascade — response shape
returns the proposal status tree with cascade-applied dispositions
CascadeJournal — change record
journals a change before transition; rollback restores
rollback restores reason field
first-touch only: second journal call is a no-op
CascadeJournal — queue membership
rollback re-inserts a removed change
rollback removes an inserted change
CascadeJournal — taxa store
rollback restores an edge after addEdge
rollback restores an edge after removeEdge
rollback removes an inserted taxon
rollback restores a renamed taxon
empty journal rollback is a no-op
CascadeJournal — full sequence
rollback restores byte-identical state across multiple mutations
runCase1Cascade
cascades from a rejected add-create to direct latent descendants
cascades from an invalid add-create with 'became invalid' verb
chains transitively when descendants are themselves add-*
op-restricted at top: rename M does not cascade
op-restricted at top: detach M does not cascade
short-circuits on already-terminal descendants
does not transition M itself
scoped to M's proposal only
uses listChangesForProposal (does not consult live state)
recursion does not re-mark already-cascaded descendants
POST /changes/:id/dismiss — auth
null caller → 403 forbidden
unknown change id → 404 change-not-found
non-reviewer → 403 not-reviewer
POST /changes/:id/dismiss — disposition preconditions
queued → 409 change-not-dismissible
accepted → 409 change-not-dismissible
rejected → 409 change-not-dismissible
POST /changes/:id/dismiss — success path
invalid-in-queue change is removed from queue; disposition unchanged
response status tree matches GET /proposals/{id}
POST /changes/:id/dismiss — lock discipline
dismiss handler does not import invariant-engine, invalidation walk, or case1-cascade
runSideEffectWalk
invalidates a queued rename whose target no longer exists
leaves still-valid queued changes alone
case-2: review-accept by reviewer auto-dismisses their other queued change
case-3: review-accept by non-reviewer leaves invalid change in queue
direct action by reviewer of affected change is case 3, not case 2
case-insensitive actor-reviewer match for case-2
skips recentlyTransitioned changes
walks across proposals (cross-proposal invalidation)
invalidates add-create with sibling name clash and propagates case-1 to descendants
skips changes whose disposition is not queued (e.g., already-invalid)
does not enter withWriteLock
runAcceptPreconditions: rename
passes when target taxon exists and name is well-formed
fails with taxon-not-found when target missing
fails defensively on bad name format
runAcceptPreconditions: add-graft
passes when parent and child exist and edge absent
fails with taxon-not-found when parent missing
fails with taxon-not-found when grafted child missing
fails with edge-already-present when graft edge exists
runAcceptPreconditions: add-create
passes when parent exists and name is well-formed
fails with taxon-not-found when parent missing
fails defensively on bad name format
resolves to-create payload-parent via store helper
runAcceptPreconditions: detach
passes when edge exists
fails with taxon-not-found when parent missing
fails with taxon-not-found when target missing
fails with edge-not-found when edge missing
promotion on add-create accept
rename direct dependent under add-create whose existence dep is the edge from the create
promotion is one level (grandchildren stay latent)
grandchildren remain latent until parent accept
promotion: rename and detach accepts do not trigger promotion
rename accept does not promote anything
promotion: existence-dep failure → invalid
descendant whose existence dep fails goes invalid at promotion
promotion: deferred reviewer resolves to acceptedBy
nested add-create's reviewer becomes the accepting reviewer
POST /changes/:id/reject: auth + precondition triplet
null caller → 403
unknown change id → 404 change-not-found
non-reviewer → 403 not-reviewer
latent change → 409 change-not-queued
POST /changes/:id/reject: body validation
no body → 200, default reason
empty JSON {} → 200, default reason
reason string → 200, stored verbatim
empty-string reason → 200, stored as empty string
extra fields ignored
non-string reason → 400 payload-shape
body is array → 400 malformed-body
body is non-JSON → 400 malformed-body
POST /changes/:id/reject: per-op cascade behavior
reject rename → only the one change transitions; no cascade
reject add-create cascades to direct dependents
reject add-create cascades transitively (grandchildren invalid)
reject add-graft cascades to descendants
cascaded-invalid changes do NOT enter the queue
POST /changes/:id/reject: live graph untouched
reject does not mutate live taxa or edges
reject handler source does not call invariant-engine
POST /changes/:id/reject: response shape
response status field matches GET /proposals/{id}
route mount: POST /changes/:id/accept
null caller receives 403
unknown id with registered caller returns 404
route mount: POST /changes/:id/reject
null caller receives 403
unknown id with registered caller returns 404
